title: allium rotundum subsp. scaberrimum
intro: Allium rotundum subsp. scaberrimum, also known as the broad-leaved chives or scabrous wild garlic, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Allium rotundum subsp. scaberrimum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
